---
title: "Project proposal"
author: "Team name"
output: html_document
---

```{r load-packages, message = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
```

## 1. Data

The data set contains sub-hourly sap flow measurements recorded from 3–4 tree species at a forested field site. Each observation includes a time stamp, a species identifier, and an inner and outer sap flow rate. Before analysis, sub-hourly readings will be aggregated to daily means to reduce high-frequency noise while preserving seasonal trends. Missing values and any sensor dropout periods will be identified and removed prior to modeling.

## 2. Exploratory Visualization

Three visualizations are planned. First, violin plots or boxplots of daily mean sap flow grouped by species will reveal whether baseline water transport rates differ across species. Second, a time series of sap flow for each species across the growing season will highlight phenological differences and whether species diverge in water use at particular times of year. Third, a plot of sap flow variability (e.g., standard deviation or interquartile range) by species will help assess whether some species are more consistent in their water transport than others — a pattern that can be just as ecologically informative as differences in mean rates.

## 3. Possible question and method

Do tree species differ significantly in their sap flow rates across the growing season?

Aggregate the sub-hourly observations into daily averages and then compare to other trees. Can also use the plots to visualize
